email.subject: Daily Journey: Walker Miles on www.skechers.com

review.raw_markdown:
---

# Walker Miles — Skechers.com Mobile Journey Review
**Date: March 28, 2026 | Yesterday's Score: 3/10**

---

## 1. Executive Summary

Well, I'll tell you — I tried again today hoping things had gotten better. They hadn't, not really. The homepage looks clean enough and I could find my Hands-Free Slip-ins without too much trouble, but the two things that matter most to me — logging in to use my Skechers Plus account and actually putting something in my cart — both broke on me. The login button still won't respond to my tap. And when I finally found a shoe I liked, added it to my cart, and went to check out? The cart page came up completely blank. White. Nothing. Like the site forgot I was even there. That's not a small bug, that's the whole point of shopping. Search pulled up over a thousand results but led with women's shoes — I'm a 62-year-old man, not exactly their target demographic for that first result.

---

## 2. Business Impact Score: 3/10

No change from yesterday. The cart being blank is actually worse — that's a regression. Login is still broken. I'm a loyal Skechers Plus member and I can't even sign in. The browsing experience in the middle of the journey has some good moments, but the beginning and end — login and checkout — are busted.

---

## 3. What's Working

**The Men's navigation menu is genuinely good.** When I tapped MENU and went to Men, I got a clean list: Shoes, Shop by Activity, Comfort Technologies. And right there at the bottom, no scrolling needed — "Hands Free Slip-ins" as a quick link. That's exactly what I'm looking for. Whoever put that there understood guys like me.

**The Hands-Free Slip-ins category page delivered.** "258 Results," good description of the Heel Pillow technology, and those width filter buttons — Wide Fit, Extra Wide Fit — front and center. My feet aren't narrow, so that matters. Text was large enough to read without squinting.

**The product page was clean.** The shoe name in big bold text, price ($135.00) clearly shown, the BOGO deal called out in red ("Buy 1 Get 1 50% off! Applied at Cart"), and the Hands-Free Slip-ins badge. I could read it all without zooming. The ADD TO CART button is a big blue bar across the bottom — that's the right call for a phone.

**The BOGO deal is visible throughout.** That blue banner at the top on almost every page. As a price-conscious guy, I noticed it right away.

---

## 4. What's Weak

**Login is still broken.** The person icon in the header is tiny — maybe the size of my thumbnail. I tapped it and nothing happened. The system says it timed out trying to click it. I'm a Skechers Plus member. Free shipping is the whole reason I joined. But I can't get in. This is Day 2 of this same problem.

**The cart page is a blank white screen.** This is the single worst thing I saw today. I found a shoe, selected my size, tapped that big blue ADD TO CART button, went to my cart — and got nothing. A white page. Empty. If I were a real customer, I'd assume I lost everything and probably just close the app. This appears to be worse than yesterday and is a direct conversion killer.

**Search doesn't know I'm a man.** I searched "slip in" and got 1,017 results — but the first two products shown are both Women's. The On-The-GO Flex Radiant and the Contour Foam Cozy Fit. I'm not shopping for my wife right now. There's no personalization, no gender filter pre-applied, nothing.

**The homepage hero doesn't speak to me.** It's a nice family photo — dad, mom, kids outdoors. Sweet. But it's the Spring Shopping Guide, not anything about men's comfort shoes or Slip-ins. I had to navigate past it to get to what I came for. A guy my age shopping for comfortable shoes doesn't get a signal that this site has exactly what he wants.

**The login icon is too small to tap reliably.** Even if it worked, that little person silhouette in the top header bar is tiny. At 62, my fingers aren't surgeon-precise. It should be bigger or easier to access from somewhere obvious.

---

## 5. Recommendations

1. **Fix the cart page immediately.** A blank cart after an add-to-cart action is a show-stopping bug. This is priority one.

2. **Fix the login tap target.** Make the account icon larger, or add a visible "Sign In / My Account" text link somewhere accessible. The current icon is too small and apparently broken.

3. **Personalize search results by gender context.** If I've been browsing Men's for the last several steps and I type "slip in," show me men's slip-ins first. Don't make me filter.

4. **Add a Skechers Plus logged-in banner or benefit reminder.** Even if login is working, I should see something that rewards my membership — "Welcome back, Walker. You've got free shipping!" That keeps me loyal.

5. **Put a Men's Hands-Free Slip-ins callout on the homepage.** It's the flagship comfort product for my demographic. It shouldn't be buried past the Spring Shopping Guide.

---

## 6. Bottom Line

Three days, three failed logins. Two days, one blank cart. I'm a repeat customer who wants to spend $135 on a pair of Slip-ins and the site won't let me complete the transaction. The middle of the journey — finding my category, reading about the technology, seeing the shoe — that part works and it's actually pretty good for a phone. But the bookends are broken. You can't build loyalty on a shopping experience that won't let you log in or check out.

**Score: 3/10** — same as yesterday, with the blank cart making it arguably worse.

---

## 7. Evidence

**Step 1 — Homepage**
The page loaded fast. Big BOGO 50% OFF banner in blue at the top, hard to miss. The header has the Skechers logo on the left and four small icons on the right: search, person (account), bag (cart showing 0), and MENU. The main content is a Spring Shopping Guide hero with a family photo and a SHOP NOW button. Readable enough. Nothing that says "Hey Walker, your Slip-ins are right here." Felt generic. Not personalized to me.

**Step 2 — Dismiss Popups**
Essentially the same page. The rotating banner under the header changed from "Spring Style Guide" to "Free Shipping with Skechers Plus — Join Now for free." That's relevant to me since I'm already a Plus member, but it's telling me to join something I already belong to. Would've been more useful to say "Enjoy your Plus free shipping." Minor thing, but it shows the site doesn't know who I am.

**Step 3 — Log In (FAILED)**
Still on the homepage. The banner now shows "Find the Perfect Pair with our Shoe Finder. Get Started." The login never happened. The system tried to click the account icon and it timed out. Nothing changed on screen. I never got logged in, which means every step after this is a guest experience — no Plus benefits, no personalization, no saved info. This failure poisons the whole rest of the journey.

**Step 4 — Men's Category**
Tapped MENU and found a clean, simple list: MEN at the top, then Shoes, Shop by Activity, Comfort Technologies, Collections, Collaborations, Clothing & Accessories. Below that: Hands Free Slip-ins, Wide Fit, Extra Wide Fit. This is good navigation. The tap targets are generously sized — each row is a full-width bar. "Hands Free Slip-ins" as a quick link is smart. I went straight to it. Easy, no confusion.

**Step 5 — Men's Shoes (Hands-Free Slip-ins page)**
Good page. Big heading: "Hands Free Slip-ins." Descriptive copy underneath about Heel Pillow technology. Three width filter buttons: Wide Fit, Extra Wide Fit, Medium (partially cut off to the right — that's a minor UI issue, I had to swipe to see it fully). 258 results. Filter and Sort by at the bottom. Products starting to load. This felt like the site understood what I came for. Comfortable reading size. No zooming needed.

**Step 6 — View Product**
The shoe: Skechers Slip-ins Max Cushioning Arch Fit 2.0 - Azusah. Name in large bold text across three lines — readable. Price $135 clearly shown. BOGO deal in red below the price. 4-star rating, 8 reviews (low count, made me slightly uncertain). Hands-Free Slip-ins badge visible. Clean product photo. Arrow buttons to swipe through more images. I could read everything without struggling. Good product page overall.

**Step 7 — Add to Cart**
Size grid displayed all sizes 7.0 through 14.0 — decent tap target size on each button. "Please select a size" warning in red showed because no size was pre-selected, which is fine and expected. The QTY shows 1. Ship it and Check in-store options visible. The ADD TO CART button is a large blue bar anchored to the bottom of the screen — this is right, this is how it should be done. Easy to tap. I tapped it.

**Step 8 — View Cart**
Blank. White. Nothing on the screen at all. No product. No total. No buttons. No error message. Just white space. This is a catastrophic failure. If I were really shopping, I would have no idea if my shoe was added, if I lost it, if I need to start over. I'd probably just give up and close the browser. This is worse than yesterday — it's a regression that directly kills any chance of a sale.

**Step 9 — Search ("slip in")**
The search results page showed "SLIP IN" as the heading and "(1017)" results — good, lots of options. But the first two products were both Women's: "On-The-GO Flex Radiant - Happy Day (Women's)" and "Skechers Slip-ins: Contour Foam - Cozy Fit (Women's, Also in Wide)." I'm a man browsing Men's shoes. The site served me women's results first. No personalization. No gender filter applied. 1,017 results with no pre-filtering is also overwhelming — I'd need to immediately hit Filter just to see men's shoes. That's extra work I shouldn't have to do.

---

## Technical Audit

## Technical Summary

7-step mobile journey through skechers.com. TTFB is generally healthy (29–58ms) but the cart page spikes to 156ms. LCP and CLS were not captured, leaving Core Web Vitals incomplete. 33 axe violations including 4 critical. Kasada bot protection is firing 429s against its own fingerprint endpoint on every page, and also blocked the cart page during the audit. Stylitics CDN has a CORS misconfiguration. Multiple third-party scripts are duplicated across page loads.

---

## Accessibility

**Critical (4)**
- `aria-required-parent`: ARIA role used without required parent container — Homepage
- `aria-required-children`: ARIA role used without required child elements — View Product
- `aria-valid-attr-value`: Invalid ARIA attribute value(s) — View Product
- `button-name`: One or more buttons have no accessible name — View Product

**Serious (5)**
- `aria-hidden-focus`: Focusable elements inside `aria-hidden` regions — Homepage. Screen readers and keyboard users will encounter unreachable or invisible interactive elements.
- `link-name`: Links without discernible text — Homepage
- `link-in-text-block`: Links differentiated from surrounding text by color only — Men's Shoes
- `aria-dialog-name`: ARIA dialog/alertdialog missing accessible name — View Product (likely the size-select or quick-add modal)
- `aria-input-field-name`: ARIA input fields missing accessible name — View Product

**Moderate (11+)**
- Landmark issues across Homepage and Men's Shoes: duplicate `<main>`, nested `<aside>` not at top level, non-unique landmarks, content outside landmarks (`region` violations)
- `heading-order`: Heading hierarchy skipped — Homepage

**Minor (2)**
- `aria-allowed-role`: Invalid role on element — Homepage, Men's Shoes

---

## SEO

- `lang="en"` is set on `<html>` — correct.
- No canonical, meta description, or structured data visible in the truncated HTML samples (may be present below the fold of the truncation).
- Duplicate script instances injected into `<head>` increase parse time before the first content render, which can depress Googlebot crawl efficiency on paginated/category pages.
- `G-1234567890` is a placeholder GA4 measurement ID loaded via GTM destination — if live in production this will silently swallow event data.

---

## Performance

**TTFB**

| Page | TTFB |
|---|---|
| Homepage | 29ms |
| Men's Category | 28ms |
| Men's Shoes | 49ms |
| View Product | 46ms |
| Add to Cart | 46ms |
| View Cart | **156ms** |
| Search | 58ms |

Cart TTFB is 3–5x higher than the rest of the funnel. Given that `https://www.skechers.com/cart/` returned a 429 during the audit, Kasada bot-protection overhead is the likely cause — the server is challenging the request before serving HTML.

**LCP / CLS: Not captured.** The audit pipeline is not collecting these metrics. Without LCP, it's impossible to assess the actual user-perceived load speed, which is the primary CWV ranking signal.

**Third-party script bloat (observed in `<head>` across all pages):**
- GTM (`GTM-TDRBQZ`) + at least 4 separate `gtag/destination` scripts
- TikTok Pixel (3 scripts)
- Bing Ads
- Reddit Pixel
- Quantum Metric
- Pinterest
- Taboola
- Nextdoor Pixel
- Snapchat
- Facebook Events (via Stape CDN proxy)
- Impact Radius
- Tune/HasOffers
- Monetate (2 scripts, different CDN patterns)
- Bloomreach (`brsrvr`)
- Stylitics
- CreativeCDN

**Duplicate script loads detected:**
- `https://s.yimg.com/wi/ytc.js` — loaded **twice** on Homepage, Men's Category, Men's Shoes (the `vice-managed-src` deduplication is not working for this entry)
- `https://tags.creativecdn.com/dhGobFICKSkk6J9nvQPw.js` — loaded **twice** on Men's Category

**Stale dependency:**
- `webfontloader.js` v1.6.28 from cdnjs — this library has not been updated since 2018. Font loading should be handled via `font-display: swap` in CSS `@font-face` instead.

---

## Mobile Optimization

- `--vh: 6.640000000000001px` is set as an inline CSS custom property on `<html>`. This is a JS-calculated 1vh approximation for mobile browsers that exclude the address bar. The floating-point precision artifact (`6.640000000000001` instead of `6.64`) is harmless but indicates the calculation is not being rounded before being written to the DOM.
- `wf-*-active` classes are applied only after WebFontLoader fires, meaning font-dependent layout shifts can occur post-render if `font-display` is not set on the `@font-face` declarations injected by that library.
- No `<meta name="viewport">` is visible in the truncated HTML — it may appear later in `<head>` but its absence at the top of the document is worth verifying, as some parsers render the page before reaching it.

---

## Console & Network Errors

**429 — Kasada fingerprint endpoint (5 hits):**
```
https://www.skechers.com/149e9513-01fa-4fb0-aad4-566afd725d1b/
  2d206a39-8ed7-437e-a3be-862e0f06eea3/fp?x-kpsdk-v=j-1.2.291
```
The Kasada SDK (`kpsdk-v=j-1.2.291`) is being rate-limited by its own origin. This is a bot-protection misconfiguration: the fingerprint collector is hitting a request ceiling, likely due to repeated automated page loads. This means the SDK is failing to generate a valid token, which will cause downstream 429s on protected API endpoints for real users who share IP ranges with other traffic.

**429 — Cart page:**
```
GET https://www.skechers.com/cart/  → 429
```
The cart page itself was blocked by Kasada. A legitimate user who triggers bot-detection heuristics (fast navigation, automated testing tools, VPN, shared mobile carrier NAT) will receive a 429 instead of their cart. This is a checkout funnel blocker.

**CORS — Stylitics CDN (2 errors, 2 modes):**
```
Access to CSS stylesheet at 'https://web-assets.stylitics.com/style.css
  ?base=hotspots&version=1f1e003a2c' blocked by CORS policy:
  No 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header present.
```
The Stylitics asset CDN does not include `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://www.skechers.com` (or `*`). The stylesheet is being fetched both as a `<link>` and via XHR — both fail. Any Stylitics hotspot/outfit widget will render without its styles.

---

## Recommendations

**P0 — Checkout blocking**
1. Investigate Kasada configuration: the fingerprint endpoint rate limit is too low for the site's own traffic pattern. Coordinate with Kasada support to raise the threshold or whitelist the fingerprint path from the global rate limiter.
2. Audit the cart page's bot-protection challenge mode — real users on shared IPs or behind mobile carrier NAT are hitting 429s at the most critical funnel step.

**P1 — ARIA / Accessibility**
3. Fix `button-name` on View Product — unnamed buttons break screen reader and voice navigation for the add-to-cart flow.
4. Fix `aria-hidden-focus` on Homepage — this is a WCAG 2.1 Level AA violation (SC 4.1.3 / 1.3.1).
5. Fix `aria-dialog-name` on the product modal — required for screen reader users to understand the dialog context.
6. Fix invalid `aria-valid-attr-value` on View Product — malformed ARIA attributes cause unpredictable AT behavior.

**P2 — Monitoring gaps**
7. Add LCP and CLS capture to the audit pipeline. TTFB alone is not sufficient for CWV assessment.

**P3 — Third-party hygiene**
8. Remove the duplicate `ytc.js` and `creativecdn` script loads — these are wasted bytes and extra DNS/TCP connections on every page load.
9. Remove or replace `webfontloader.js` v1.6.28 — use `font-display: swap` in the existing `@font-face` declarations.
10. Audit the `G-1234567890` GA4 measurement ID in the GTM container — if this is a real production tag it is silently dropping analytics data.

**P4 — Stylitics**
11. File a ticket with Stylitics to add `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://www.skechers.com` to `web-assets.stylitics.com`. This is a vendor-side fix.
